home.headline: Democracy Cannot Be Jailed.
home.subheadline: The people's mandate is under attack. 16 million citizens cast their ballot. One loyalist court unlawfully imprisoned their choice. This is the anatomical record of a judicial coup against Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu.
home.quote: "When you cannot win by the ballot, you weaponize the bench. This is not about my freedom. This is about the extinction of yours."
home.theCaseSummary: A forensic breakdown of the legal proceedings. Discover how a fabricated interpretation of a single remark was weaponized by loyalist prosecutors to execute a judicial coup against the government's strongest rival.

annulment.title: The Annulment
annulment.text: The Supreme Election Council controversially cancelled İmamoğlu's victory under immense government pressure. His diploma was questioned in an attempt to block his future presidential eligibility. The next morning, detention followed.

elections.title: He Kept Winning
elections.subtitle: Each attempt to weaken his legitimacy only strengthened his public mandate.

latest.title: Latest Developments
latest.summary: Tracking the ongoing legal maneuvers and political counter-strikes.

actions.title: Public Action & Resistance
actions.summary: The civic response to judicial suppression.

world.title: Why The World Should Care
world.text: This case extends far beyond a single mayor. It is a critical stress test for the survival of the rule of law and democratic opposition in Turkey. If the electoral will of 16 million citizens in Europe's largest city can be erased by court decree, the blueprint for authoritarian entrenchment is complete.

cta.title: Stand With The Mandate
cta.text: Track the case. Share the evidence. Coordinate international monitoring.

theCase.title: The Political Case
theCase.summary: A forensic breakdown of the legal proceedings.

footer.disclaimer: This independent platform documents the ongoing judicial suppression of democratic rights in Turkey. Silence is complicity.
